decision_making_reasoning: The current grid state shows a stable monotonic wall in the bottom row (1024, 512, 256, 128), anchored by the 1024 tile in the bottom-right corner. There is no trapped tile, and the new '2' in the top-left does not interfere with merges. The goal is to merge the two highest-value tiles — currently 1024 and 512 — which are adjacent horizontally in the bottom row. To achieve this, we must move right, which will push the 512 tile into the 1024 tile, triggering a merge. This preserves the anchor lock, increases the score, and maintains the monotonic structure. No other move (up, down, left) would enable this merge without breaking the wall or risking instability.

The last action was move_right(), which likely consolidated or maintained this wall. This move does not disturb the bottom row’s structure and may create space or merges elsewhere without risking the anchor. No other direction (up, down, right) would better serve the goal of consolidating the monotonic structure — moving right might push tiles into the anchored wall unnecessarily, and up/down could break the row's integrity.
